title: tephrosia pumila
intro: Tephrosia pumila, also known as the dwarf tephrosia or dwarf hoary pea, is a low-growing, woody perennial plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Tephrosia pumila include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia pumila include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
